rationale: Both correctly identify the same root cause (non-async-chunk branch shipping (None, payload) instead of the full payload, starving the downstream stage) and the same fix/PR #4792/0.24-rebase resolution, matching the ground truth thread closely. X is somewhat more grounded — it traces the full causal chain (_FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES registration, accumulate_full_payload_output gating, coordinator/scheduler timeout log lines) and explicitly triages red herrings in the reporter's env dump (NaN-clamp patch, min_tokens/#4962), showing deeper engagement with the actual issue body. Y's answer is solid but cites a verification test (test_sample_tokens_tail_only_prefix_cache_uses_staged_cpu_hidden_states) that has nothing to do with inter-stage payload routing, which is an ungrounded/non-sequitur claim that slightly undercuts its credibility. Completeness is close, with Y crediting the reporter's already-confirmed workaround slightly better.
